exhibits.description: Knife recovered from accused Ramesh Kumar at time of arrest on 15.01.2024
Blood-stained kurta of complainant Sita Devi
MLC Report from RML Hospital (MLC No. RML/2024/1234) - 3cm laceration right forearm, simple hurt

key_facts.fact: FIR registered at 22:45 hrs on 14.01.2024; alleged offence at approximately 10:00-10:30 hrs on same date — gap of approximately 12 hours 15 minutes, which crosses the legally significant 12-hour threshold per Supreme Court precedents and is acknowledged in FIR itself

Complainant Sita Devi arrived at RML Hospital Emergency at 14:30 hrs (per MLC in chargesheet), which is 4-5 hours after the alleged offence time of 10:00-10:30 hrs — unexplained delay in seeking medical treatment

FIR states complainant returned home at 9:30 PM and only then filed complaint at PS at 22:45 hrs after daughter's insistence — approximately 7 hours after hospital discharge, no explanation for this further delay
FIR states accused arrested on 15.01.2024 at 16:30 hrs; chargesheet states arrest at approximately 14:00 hrs — direct contradiction of 2.5 hours in arrest time

FIR invokes BNS Section 103 citing 'threat to kill'; chargesheet invokes BNS Section 103 for attempted culpable homicide amounting to murder — injury noted is only 3cm laceration classified as 'simple hurt', making Section 103 application legally tenuous

FIR charges BNS Section 316 (Cheating and Extortion); chargesheet charges BNS Section 308 (Extortion) — section changed without explanation; BNS 316 does not exist as 'cheating and extortion' — possible error in FIR section citation

Chargesheet filed in Saket District Courts before Additional Sessions Judge Suresh Verma, but FIR registered at PS Connaught Place under Central Delhi District — jurisdictional discrepancy between court mentioned and PS jurisdiction

Knife (MO-1) recovered at arrest on 15.01.2024, approximately 28 hours after alleged offence — no independent witness to recovery; raises questions about chain of custody and whether weapon was planted

MLC classifies injury as 'simple hurt' — 3cm laceration on right forearm treated and discharged — inconsistent with prosecution's invocation of BNS Section 103 (attempted murder) and Section 115 (hurt by dangerous weapon)

FIR states accused attacked complainant with 'sharp weapon'; chargesheet identifies weapon as knife (MO-1) — weapon not described or identified at FIR stage, identity of weapon introduced only post-arrest
FIR states accused arrested at Ajmeri Gate from 'his residence'; chargesheet states arrest based on 'informer's tip' from Ajmeri Gate area — discrepancy in how accused was located for arrest

Accused produced before Metropolitan Magistrate, Patiala House Courts on 16.01.2024 per FIR; chargesheet states remanded to 14-day judicial custody — Patiala House Courts is inconsistent with PS Connaught Place which typically falls under Tis Hazari/Patiala House jurisdiction; requires verification
FIR states injury was on complainant's 'right arm'; MLC and chargesheet specify 'right forearm' — minor inconsistency in body part description
Eyewitness PW-3 Rajendra Prasad (shopkeeper at adjacent Shop No. 22) listed in chargesheet but not mentioned anywhere in FIR — his existence and presence not disclosed at FIR stage
FIR states alleged offence time as '10:00-10:30 hrs'; chargesheet narrows it to '10:15 hours' — precision introduced in chargesheet without basis in FIR
Accused's occupation stated as 'labourer' in chargesheet — relevant to credibility of extortion demand of Rs. 50,000 and whether accused had means or criminal network to demand hafta
Chargesheet submitted on 25.01.2024 — 11 days after FIR; within statutory period but timeline of investigation should be verified against BNSS 2023 requirements
Medical examination of accused Ramesh Kumar conducted at LNJP Hospital post-arrest — report not exhibited or referenced in chargesheet exhibits list

witnesses.name: Dr. Priya Mehta, RML Hospital (Medical Officer who treated complainant)
Rajendra Prasad (Eyewitness, shopkeeper at adjacent Shop No. 22)
Constable Sunil Kumar (Accompanied IO during arrest)

contradictions.description: CRITICAL — Arrest time conflict: FIR states accused arrested at 16:30 hrs on 15.01.2024; chargesheet states arrest at approximately 14:00 hrs on 15.01.2024 — a 2.5-hour discrepancy which may affect legality of arrest, remand computation and custody period calculation under BNSS 2023

CRITICAL — Sections charged: FIR invokes BNS Section 316 (described as 'Cheating and Extortion'); chargesheet charges BNS Section 308 (Extortion) — unexplained change in section without amendment to FIR; BNS 316 cited in FIR appears to be a non-existent or incorrectly cited provision for the alleged act

CRITICAL — BNS Section 103 application vs. medical evidence: Prosecution invokes BNS 103 (attempted murder/culpable homicide) but MLC conclusively classifies injury as 'simple hurt' with only 3cm laceration — MLC evidence directly contradicts the gravest charge; no dangerous weapon injury consistent with attempted murder documented

SIGNIFICANT — Eyewitness PW-3 Rajendra Prasad not mentioned in FIR at all despite being shopkeeper at adjacent Shop No. 22 who would have been immediately accessible — his introduction only at chargesheet stage raises credibility concerns about his claimed presence and testimony

SIGNIFICANT — Hospital arrival time: FIR states complainant returned home at 9:30 PM implying she was at hospital most of the day; MLC shows she arrived at RML at 14:30 hrs — this means complainant was at hospital only briefly (treated and discharged) but did not report to police for another ~8 hours after discharge, unexplained in both documents

MODERATE — Manner of arrest: FIR states accused arrested from 'his residence' at Ajmeri Gate; chargesheet states arrest was based on 'informer's tip' from Ajmeri Gate area — discrepancy in how accused was located raises questions about pre-arrest surveillance, whether arrest was pre-planned, and whether proper arrest procedure under BNSS 2023 was followed

MODERATE — Court jurisdiction: Chargesheet filed before Additional Sessions Judge at Saket District Courts; FIR registered at PS Connaught Place (Central District) — Connaught Place PS does not fall under Saket courts jurisdiction; this raises a jurisdictional issue regarding the proper court for trial

MODERATE — Offence time precision: FIR states offence occurred at 'approximately 10:00-10:30 hrs'; chargesheet states 'approximately 10:15 hours' — artificial precision introduced in chargesheet without factual basis disclosed in FIR or investigation documents

MINOR — Body part description: FIR states injury on 'right arm'; MLC and chargesheet specify 'right forearm' — while forearm is part of the arm, inconsistency in anatomical description could affect credibility of complainant's account in FIR

MINOR — Weapon description: FIR describes 'sharp weapon' generically; chargesheet specifically identifies knife (MO-1) recovered from accused 28 hours post-offence — identification of specific weapon introduced only post-arrest, not by complainant or eyewitness in FIR

missing_docs: Arrest memo of accused Ramesh Kumar (mentioned in FIR but not exhibited in chargesheet — mandatory under BNSS 2023 Section 35)

Medical examination report of accused Ramesh Kumar from LNJP Hospital post-arrest (mentioned in FIR but absent from chargesheet exhibits — critical to establish accused's condition and rule out false implication)
Recovery memo / seizure memo for MO-2 (blood-stained kurta of complainant) — not exhibited separately; chain of custody document missing
Forensic Science Laboratory (FSL) report for MO-1 (knife) — blood group matching, fingerprint analysis not referenced
FSL report for MO-2 (blood-stained kurta) — blood group and DNA analysis absent
Section 27 Indian Evidence Act / BSA 2023 disclosure statement of accused leading to recovery of knife (MO-1) — essential for admissibility of recovery
CCTV footage from Palika Bazaar Shop No. 23 or surrounding area — not referenced in either document despite Connaught Place being a CCTV-dense area
Scene of crime panchnama / spot inspection report for Shop No. 22-23, Palika Bazaar
Statement of Rajendra Prasad (PW-3 eyewitness) recorded under BNSS Section 180 during investigation — not referenced in chargesheet
Call detail records (CDR) of accused and complainant around time of alleged offence
Remand application and remand order from Metropolitan Magistrate, Patiala House Courts dated 16.01.2024
Section 164 BNSS 2023 statement of complainant Sita Devi recorded before Magistrate
IO's case diary entries from 14.01.2024 to 25.01.2024 covering the entire investigation period
MLC of accused Ramesh Kumar from LNJP Hospital (mentioned in FIR but not exhibited in chargesheet)
Shop ownership or tenancy documents for Shop No. 23, Palika Bazaar to establish complainant's presence and business
Identification parade proceedings if any, for accused identification by PW-3 (eyewitness who was not present at FIR stage)
